body:
- Upgraded Spring Boot Version to 4.0.0
- Upgraded Maven and Gradle Wrappers
- Upgraded to current PostgreSql and MySql containers
- Minor dependency upgrades
- Removed unused imports
- Replaced deprecated MySQLContainer
- Using new property for spring.web.error.include-message

comments.body:
I just realized that Spring Boot 4.0.0.RC2 uses a different docker-java library which is noch compatible with the API of the newest Docker Desktop 4.52.0 -> Build failures 

Upgrading to Spring Boot 4.0.0 solves the issue.

Good morning @dsyer, please have a look at my Spring Boot 4.0 updates. 

TIA
I am going to revert the removal of `--release` on 17. While we obviously recommend the latest LTS, there's no reason to prevent users to run petclinic on an older, supported, JDK.

Sorry, @snicoll, removing the release flag. I thought cleaning up is a good idea since the `java.version` change was introduced by #2016 to solve an issue with NullAway. This is not the case anymore, and building with Java 17 works correctly. We could also set the `java.version` back to Java 17 again.

title: Upgrade to Spring Boot 4.0.0
